From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian noblewoman and mistress of Lorenzo de' Medici

| Lucrezia Donati | |
| --- | --- |
| Detail of the head of the painting *[Fortitude](/wiki/Fortitude_(Botticelli) "Fortitude (Botticelli)")*, by [Sandro Botticelli](/wiki/Sandro_Botticelli "Sandro Botticelli"). It is hypothesized that the painting was inspired by Lucrezia. | |
| [Coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms "Coat of arms") |  |
| Born | 1447 (1447) [Florence](/wiki/Florence "Florence"), Italy |
| Died | 1501 (aged 53–54)  Florence, Italy |
| Spouse | Niccolò Ardighelli |
| Father | Manno Donati |
| Mother | Caterina Bardi |

**Lucrezia Donati** [(Florence](/wiki/Florence "Florence"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), 1447–1501) was an Italian noblewoman, and [mistress](/wiki/Mistress_(lover) "Mistress (lover)") of [Lorenzo de' Medici](/wiki/Lorenzo_de%27_Medici "Lorenzo de' Medici").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The identity of the woman in the sculpture *[Dama col mazzolino](/wiki/Dama_col_mazzolino "Dama col mazzolino")*, at [Bargello Museum](/wiki/Bargello_Museum "Bargello Museum") in Florence has been attributed by some to Lucrezia Donati.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENifosì201646-5)[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENifosì20169-10-6) Also [Sandro Botticelli](/wiki/Sandro_Botticelli "Sandro Botticelli")'s painting *[Fortitude](/wiki/Fortitude_(Botticelli) "Fortitude (Botticelli)")* would be another representation of her.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucrezia_Donati&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Lucrezia was the daughter of Manno Donati and Caterina Bardi, a Florentine [dame](/wiki/Dame "Dame")[[9]](#cite_note-Walter-9) who belonged to a family tree of which she became the last descendant.

From 1461 she was the mistress of [Lorenzo il Magnifico](/wiki/Lorenzo_il_Magnifico "Lorenzo il Magnifico"),[[9]](#cite_note-Walter-9) a [platonic love](/wiki/Platonic_love "Platonic love") affair,[[10]](#cite_note-10) until Lorenzo later married the Italian noble [Clarice Orsini](/wiki/Clarice_Orsini "Clarice Orsini") in 1469.[[11]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEVannucci1999-11) In 1486 Lorenzo remembered the poems he had written for her when he was 16 in the poem *Corinto*.

Lucrezia married the Florentian businessman Niccolò Ardighelli, who died [in exile](/wiki/Exile "Exile") in 1496.[[9]](#cite_note-Walter-9)

## Popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucrezia_Donati&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Popular culture")]

The actress [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock") starred as Lucrezia Donati in the [television series](/wiki/Television_series "Television series") *[Da Vinci's Demons](/wiki/Da_Vinci%27s_Demons "Da Vinci's Demons")*.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) [Alessandra Mastronardi](/wiki/Alessandra_Mastronardi "Alessandra Mastronardi") played Lucrezia in *[Medici: The Magnificent](/wiki/Medici:_Masters_of_Florence "Medici: Masters of Florence")*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
